visual_attributes.morphology: vertically oriented rotating column of wind
distinctive_features: visible dust or sand particles caught in the vortex
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: ranges from 18 inches (0.5 meters) to over 30 feet (10 meters) wide and up to half a mile (1 km) tall
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: central core of rising hot air surrounded by descending cooler air

domain_specific_visual.VISIBILITY: enhanced by dust or sand particles

functional_attributes.primary_function: transport dust and small particles into the atmosphere

domain_specific_functional.ELECTRICAL_ACTIVITY: produces radio noise and electrical fields
domain_specific_functional.PARTICLE_LIFTING: lifts dust and small particles through triboelectrification

relationships.description: Both are vertically oriented rotating columns of wind, but dust devils are less intense.
Similar formation process but occurs in recently burned areas.
Similar in appearance but involves fire and is more intense.

contextual_attributes.origin: occurs globally in arid and semi-arid regions
contextual_attributes.geographic_range: common in deserts and flat barren terrains
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: often seen as harmless but can be dangerous in rare cases
